steps.title: Join two independent clauses
steps.content: The first clause says the guide grew in scope and influence. The second clause says its modest French eateries were replaced by esteemed restaurants around the world. A comma plus coordinating conjunction can join them.

steps.title: Reject faulty boundaries
steps.content: Choice A gives no proper boundary before the second clause. Choice C uses only a comma, creating a comma splice. Choice D uses and without the needed comma between independent clauses. The answer is B.
